subject: Enable i2c2 device.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3, row grouping c5066763c with 13 other DTS commits (aa8afe109..077c2c317)

impact.effect_if_absent: i2c2 bus remains disabled; ADV7513 HDMI transmitter at address 0x39 cannot be probed; HDMI output silently fails to initialize with no error message

userspace_coupling.interface: i2c bus /dev/i2c-2 enumeration; ADV7513 HDMI transmitter I2C probing at address 0x39
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/smbus.cpp:214-222 — hardcoded scan limit enforces exactly 3 i2c adapters (buses 0-2); if force_bus > 2, function returns -1; Main_MiSTer/video.cpp:1448 probes 0x39 across /dev/i2c-0..2 at runtime to locate HDMI transmitter

notes: CRITICAL: i2c2 enables the third and final i2c adapter required for Main_MiSTer HDMI support. The original commit adds &i2c2 { status="okay"; clock-frequency=<100000>; speed-mode=<0>; }. The carried patch 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch includes this node but DROPS the speed-mode property (line reads &i2c2 { clock-frequency=<100000>; status="okay"; }), a re-implementation choice because: (1) speed-mode is not a recognized i2c property in 6.18 i2c bindings (Documentation/devicetree/bindings/i2c/), (2) no i2c driver reads it, and (3) would be silently ignored anyway. File path changed during 6.18 forward-port from fork's old location arch/arm/boot/dts/socfpga_cyclone5_de10_nano.dts (no vendor subdir) to 6.18 canonical path arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/socfpga_cyclone5_de10nano.dts. Grouped with 13 other DTS commits in provenance.md (one row, SHA range aa8afe109..077c2c317), but this analysis is commit-specific per Phase 1 protocol.
